description: primary project accession within which the sequencing was organised (e.g. PRJEB12345)
comments: any additional comments, can have multiple, seperated by commas

description: Name of the study
comments: any additional comments, can have multiple, seperated by commas

description: Taxonomy ID of the organism as in the NCBI Taxonomy database. Entries in the NCBI Taxonomy database have integer taxon IDs.
comments: any additional comments, can have multiple, seperated by commas

description: Scientific name of the organism as in the NCBI Taxonomy database. Scientific names typically follow the binomial nomenclature. For examle, fecal samples collected from the mouse gut is 'mouse gut metagenome' with the corresponding tax id 410661.
comments: any additional comments, can have multiple, seperated by commas

description: Unique name of the sample. If not selected system will automatically use the ENA sample accession as the sample ID
comments: any additional comments, can have multiple, seperated by commas

description: title of the sample
comments: any additional comments, can have multiple, seperated by commas

description: description of the sample
comments: any additional comments, can have multiple, seperated by commas
examples: fecal sample collected from syk mouse gut

description: The date the sample was collected with the intention of sequencing, either as an instance (single point in time) or interval. In case no exact time is available, the date/time can be right truncated i.e. all of these are valid ISO8601 compliant times: 2008-01-23T19:23:10+00:00; 2008-01-23T19:23:10; 2008-01-23; 2008-01; 2008.
comments: any additional comments, can have multiple, seperated by commas

description: start time of sample collection
comments: any additional comments, can have multiple, seperated by commas

description: end time of sample collection
comments: any additional comments, can have multiple, seperated by commas

description: amount or size of sample collected
comments: any additional comments, can have multiple, seperated by commas

description: The geographical origin of where the sample was collected from, with the intention of sequencing, as defined by the country or sea name. Country or sea names should be chosen from the INSDC country list (http://insdc.org/country.html).
comments: any additional comments, can have multiple, seperated by commas

description: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by latitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees and in WGS84 system
comments: any additional comments, can have multiple, seperated by commas

description: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by longitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees and in WGS84 system
comments: any additional comments, can have multiple, seperated by commas

description: Report the major environmental system the sample or specimen came from. The system(s) identified should have a coarse spatial grain, to provide the general environmental context of where the sampling was done (e.g. in the desert or a rainforest). We recommend using subclasses of EnvOŐs biome class: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00000428. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS.
comments: any additional comments, can have multiple, seperated by commas

description: Report the entity or entities which are in the sample or specimenŐs local vicinity and which you believe have significant causal influences on your sample or specimen. We recommend using EnvO terms which are of smaller spatial grain than your entry for "broad-scale environmental context". Terms, such as anatomical sites, from other OBO Library ontologies which interoperate with EnvO (e.g. UBERON) are accepted in this field. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS.
comments: any additional comments, can have multiple, seperated by commas

description: Report the environmental material(s) immediately surrounding the sample or specimen at the time of sampling. We recommend using subclasses of 'environmental material' (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00010483). EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS . Terms from other OBO ontologies are permissible as long as they reference mass/volume nouns (e.g. air, water, blood) and not discrete, countable entities (e.g. a tree, a leaf, a table top).
comments: any additional comments, can have multiple, seperated by commas
